name: Chocolate Cake
summary: A rich, moist sourdough-discard chocolate cake.

ingredients.item: 120ml neutral oil (e.g. canola or vegetable oil)
240ml hot water or hot coffee (coffee deepens chocolate flavor but is optional)

tips: Make sure your discard has no signs of spoilage (no pink or mold).
This cake is very forgiving — discard that's a few days old in the fridge works fine.
You can store it covered for up to 3 days, or freeze slices for later.

steps.title: Preheat Oven
steps.instruction: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9×13 inch (23×33 cm) baking pan or two 8-inch round pans.

steps.title: Mix Dry Ingredients
steps.instruction: In a large bowl, sift together flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.

steps.title: Combine Wet Ingredients
steps.instruction: In another bowl, whisk together eggs, sugar, brown sugar, milk, oil, and vanilla until well blended. Stir in the sourdough discard and fully incorporate.

steps.title: Combine Wet and Dry
steps.instruction: Gradually mix the dry ingredients into the wet until no flour remains. Slowly add the hot water (or hot coffee), mixing until smooth. The batter will be thin — this is normal.

steps.title: Bake
steps.instruction: Pour the batter into the prepared pan(s). Bake for 30–35 minutes (round pans) or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.

steps.title: Cool
steps.instruction: Allow to cool in the pan for 10–15 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

steps.title: Serve
steps.instruction: Enjoy as-is or frost with your favorite chocolate frosting once fully cooled.
